nodes.context: Italian criminal organization based in Rome, active from 1975 to 1993, involved in drug trafficking, murder, and political terrorism.
Sicilian mafia organization, heavily involved in drug trafficking and money laundering.
Neapolitan criminal organization, involved in various illegal activities.
Calabrian mafia organization, known for drug trafficking and extortion.
Italian neofascist terrorist group, responsible for the 1980 Bologna massacre.
Masonic lodge turned conspiratorial cell, involved in political and financial scandals.
Camorra organization led by Raffaele Cutolo, rival of Nuova Famiglia.
Camorra organization headed by Carmine Alfieri, rival of Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
Rival criminal group to the Banda della Magliana in Rome.

edges.context: The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with Cosa Nostra.
The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with Camorra.
The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with 'Ndrangheta.
The Banda della Magliana and NAR were closely linked, with the gang laundering NAR's money and NAR assisting in street activities.
Left-wing journalists alleged links between the Banda della Magliana and P2, but no concrete proof exists.
The NCO and NF were rival Camorra organizations, both dealing with Aldo Semerari.
The Proietti clan is listed as a rival of the Banda della Magliana.
